name: K-Way Merge — K Sorted Lists, K Closest Points

metadata.career_relevance: Merge K Sorted Lists is the canonical heap-of-heaps problem. K Closest Points is Flipkart's most common medium. Both appear in Amazon's final rounds.

hook.what_you_will_build: K-Way Merge — K Sorted Lists, K Closest Points

key_takeaways: Merge K sorted: PriorityQueue<ListNode> by val. Push heads; extract min; push next.
K Closest: max-heap of size k on distance²; evict farthest
Kth smallest in sorted matrix: min-heap starting from (0,0); push right and down
Smallest range: push first element from each list; track max; range=[min,max]
K-way merge complexity: O(N log K) — N total elements, K lists
reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Heap Synthesis — putting it all together.
